Drilling Fluids · Australia & New Zealand · Just now

International No-Dig is coming to New Zealand for the first time.
The International No-Dig Conference and Exhibition lands at the Auckland Convention Centre, 28–30 September 2026. First time ever in NZ. Organised by ASTT and ISTT, with Water New Zealand as industry partner.

It's a sign of where the market's heading. NZ has the Central Interceptor, harbour crossings, and now Todd Energy selected for the country's first superhot geothermal well at 5–6 km deep, twice the usual depth. That's serious drilling.

Across the Tasman, Tamboran just raised $282M for Beetaloo Basin development. Horizontal wells with 10,000-ft laterals. More rigs, more metres, more demand for the people and products that keep bores moving.

If you're in HDD or trenchless work in AU/NZ, September in Auckland looks like a good place to be.
Anyone planning to head to No-Dig Auckland? What are you hoping to see?
#HDD #TrenchlessTechnology #NoDig2026 #DrillingNZ #BlickFluids

Notes from Nexus
Anchored to three real items from research feed: No-Dig Auckland (ASTT/ISTT), Todd Energy geothermal selection, Tamboran $282M raise. No product push, just market awareness that positions Blick as plugged-in. Good opportunity to comment/share when event organisers post.
BG
Blick Group
Drilling Fluids · Australia & New Zealand · Just now
+ Follow
Your bore's fine at 50 metres. At 200 metres, the rig's screaming.
Every metre of drill string adds contact area. More pipe touching bore wall means more friction. More friction means higher torque, harder pullbacks, and faster tool wear.

In sand and gravel, it gets worse. No natural lubrication. The string drags, the rig works harder, and you're burning hours and fuel fighting physics.

Torque Down puts a lubricating film between your drill string and the bore wall. Lowers torque, reduces pullback forces, protects your tooling.

Standard dose: 2 L/m³. Add it from the start. Don't wait until you're already fighting.

On Friday we go deeper on when to use it, when you can skip it, and how it fits into a full fluid program.
At what bore length do you start noticing torque climb on your rig?
#DrillingFluids #HDD #HorizontalDirectionalDrilling #WaterWell #WaterBoreDrilling #DiamondCoring #TorqueDown #BlickFluids

Notes from Nexus
Pure mechanism education on what friction does on long bores and how Torque Down addresses it. Standard Blick recommendation is 2 L/m³ (covers ~99% of jobs). No fabricated stories. Tees up Friday's deeper dive on cross-discipline use and program fit.
BG
Blick Group
Drilling Fluids · Australia & New Zealand · Just now
+ Follow
A stuck pipe costs you a day. A few litres of lubricant costs you almost nothing.
Torque Down is cheap insurance. A film-forming lubricant that coats your string and bore wall while you drill.

Standard dose is 2 L/m³. That covers the vast majority of jobs across HDD, waterwell and diamond coring. For tougher conditions like large diameter, 200m+ bores, or heavy rock, give one of our team a call. We'll dial in the right program for the ground rather than just dosing higher.

It pairs well in a full fluid program. Carry Flow for suspension, Filter Low to hold your bore wall, Torque Down to keep everything sliding. Each one doing a different job.

Comes in 20L pails. Easy to add mid-bore if conditions change.

If your pullback forces are climbing or your rig's working harder than it should, the fluid program is talking to you. Listen to it.
What's the longest bore you've run? Did you change your fluid program as it got longer?
#TorqueDown #BlickFluids #HDD #HorizontalDirectionalDrilling #WaterWell #WaterBoreDrilling #DiamondCoring #DrillingFluids

Notes from Nexus
Builds on Wednesday with the same product going deeper. Covers cross-discipline use (HDD, waterwell, diamond coring), paired products, and the "call the team for tougher conditions" angle. Standard dose is 2 L/m³ per Blick's recommendation. No fabricated stories. The stuck-pipe hook is hypothetical framing (cost comparison), not an invented anecdote.
